meaning: Strait of 奥特朗托海峡
examples: In Europe, the southern Mediterranean coast of the peninsula beautiful Otranto, inlaid with a splendid pearl - Italy.
Indeed, of all the towns I visit, only Otranto has a classic beach: a veritable Miami compared to tiny Depressa and Melpignano.
William Cran, an award-winning documentary film-maker, was born in Australia in 1946 and came to this country as a small boy on the SS Otranto.
The Italian martyrs were residents of the Adriatic seaside town of Otranto.
The "Martyrs of Otranto" are 813 Italians who were slain in the southern Italian city in 1480 for defying demands by Turkish invaders to renounce Christianity.
